content.text:
<user_query>
You are acting as a reviewer for this user request. Check for blocking issues and next actions after a live sweep dispatch where dispatch summary succeeded but verify script returned unknown statuses for task `r01_v12_sa_amp_with_4dof_arms_and_head_full_scenes_stability_priority_ema` with seed-only `hp_specs`. Inspect repository context if needed for `sweep-fuyao`/`fuyao_deploy.sh` behavior and any environment constraints around verification scripts. Return: objective summary, potential blockers, needed follow-ups, and whether extra verification is required.
</user_query>

<git_status>
This is the git status at the start of the conversation. Note that this status is a snapshot in time, and will not update during the conversation.

Git repo: /Users/HanHu/software/motion_rl

M humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh
 M humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh
?? hyperparameter-suggestion.md
</git_status>

<agent_transcripts>
Agent transcripts (past chats) live in /Users/HanHu/.cursor/projects/Users-HanHu-software-motion-rl/agent-transcripts. They have names like <uuid>.jsonl, cite them to the user as [<title for chat <=6 words>](<uuid excluding .jsonl>). NEVER cite subagent transcripts/IDs; you can only cite parent uuids. Don't discuss the folder structure.
</agent_transcripts>

<agent_skills>
When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any of the available skills below can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge. To use a skill, read the skill file at the provided absolute path using the ReadFile tool, then follow the instructions within. When a skill is relevant, read and follow it IMMEDIATELY as your first action. NEVER just announce or mention a skill without actually reading and following it. Only use skills listed below.

<available_skills description="Skills the agent can use. Use the ReadFile tool with the provided absolute path to fetch full contents.">
<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/checkout-for-huh8/SKILL.md">Derive and checkout huh8-prefixed git branches while preserving already-prefixed branches, and prompt for explicit suffix when creating a new branch from a huh8 source branch. Use when the user asks to create, rename, or checkout branches with huh8 naming or company-ID branch conventions.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/commit-tag-prefix/SKILL.md">Prepends git commit messages with an angle-bracketed type tag and enforces a consistent format. Use when creating commit messages, preparing git commit text, or when the user asks to commit staged changes.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/hp-sweep-orchestrator/SKILL.md">Orchestrates hyperparameter sweep training jobs on Fuyao. Use when the user mentions hyperparameter sweep, parameter search, training sweep, sweep jobs, or wants to deploy multiple training runs with different config values.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/motion-rl-isaacgym-exec/SKILL.md">Executes shell commands inside the isaacgym container for the Motion RL workspace. Use when the user mentions Motion RL, motion_rl, isaacgym-motion_rl workspace, or asks to run commands in that workspace.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/plotjuggler-udp-fuzzy/SKILL.md">Generates and updates PlotJuggler layout XML and streaming XML using UDP JSON signals. Use when the user mentions PlotJuggler, layout files, streaming config, UDP telemetry, fuzzy signal names, or wants fuzzy-to-exact signal mapping.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/policy-lineage-tracker/SKILL.md">Interactive conversational agent for policy lineage experiment tracking. Parses natural-language requests, gathers missing context through guided questions, and calls the tracker CLI to record nodes, export mermaid diagrams, and populate lineage tables. Use when the user mentions policy lineage, experiment tracking, mutations, runs, tasks, tracker nodes, exporting lineage graphs, or describes training results.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/reward-design-analyzer/SKILL.md">Analyzes RL reward design for a registered task by tracing config inheritance, categorizing all reward terms, ranking importance, identifying external disturbances, and producing a Markdown report with tuning suggestions. Use when the user asks to analyze rewards, understand reward design, trace reward configs, tune rewards, or wants a reward breakdown for a task in the motion_rl / humanoid-gym codebase.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/sweep-fuyao/SKILL.md">Deterministic dispatch and verification of Fuyao hyperparameter sweeps.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/sync-after-skill-create/SKILL.md">Legacy fallback for toolbox sync guidance. Prefer the personal rule `~/.cursor/rules/sync-toolbox-after-toolbox-edits.mdc` for primary behavior when editing toolbox assets.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/create-rule/SKILL.md">Create Cursor rules for persistent AI guidance. Use when you want to create a rule, add coding standards, set up project conventions, configure file-specific patterns, create RULE.md files, or asks about .cursor/rules/ or AGENTS.md.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/create-skill/SKILL.md">Guides users through creating effective Agent Skills for Cursor. Use when you want to create, write, or author a new skill, or asks about skill structure, best practices, or SKILL.md format.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/update-cursor-settings/SKILL.md">Modify Cursor/VSCode user settings in settings.json. Use when you want to change editor settings, preferences, configuration, themes, font size, tab size, format on save, auto save, keybindings, or any settings.json values.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/deploy-fuyao/SKILL.md">Deterministic Fuyao deployment flow with strict validation, branch sync, and confirmation.</agent_skill>
</available_skills>
</agent_skills>

Findings-first review:

1. High blocker: the sweep is not yet actually verified as “running training”; it is only “submitted”.
- In `verification/combo_0001.json`, `job_status`/`training_status` are both `"unknown"` (hard-blocking for that combo).
- In `verification/combo_0002..0005.json`, `job_status` is `"running"` but `training_status` is `"waiting_for_logs"` and `verdict` is `"UNKNOWN"`.
- This means dispatch completion was reached, but the job evidence loop has not yet observed in-epoch training logs.

Code evidence:
```1:54:~/.cursor/scripts/verify_fuyao_jobs.sh
st="$(check_job_status "$ssh_alias" "$job")"
...
training="$(check_training_started "$ssh_alias" "$job")"
if [ "$status" = "running" ] || [ "$status" = "succeeded" ]; then ...
```
(`check_job_status` and `check_training_started` are used for status derivation and training markers).

2. High blocker: status parsing is strict and can yield `"unknown"` if `fuyao info` emits unexpected format.
- Parser only recognizes specific status tokens and exact regex `status\s*:\s*(\S+)`.
- If `fuyao info` output changed or is truncated/transient, verification returns `unknown`, even if the job exists.

Code evidence:
```83:95:~/.cursor/scripts/verify_fuyao_jobs.sh
m = re.search(r'status\s*:\s*(\S+)', info_text)
...
elif 'not found' in info_text.lower() ... status = 'not_found'
```

3. Medium blocker: remote worktree is intentionally detached in the sweep prep path, and this is correlated with the reproducibility warning in dispatch logs.
- Remote prep creates a worktree from `origin/${BRANCH}` directly, which leaves HEAD detached.
- This matches recurring log warnings: `fatal: HEAD does not point to a branch` and `Failed to detect upstream ... git rev-parse '@{u}'`.
- These are non-fatal to submit, but can undermine confidence in provenance and may affect platform-level metadata behavior.

Code evidence:
```588:588:~/.cursor/scripts/deploy_fuyao_sweep_dispatcher.sh
git worktree add --force "$COMBO_DIR" "origin/${BRANCH}" >/dev/null
```

4. Medium risk: Fuyao CLI Python-version compatibility warning appears consistently in submission logs.
- This is currently advisory, but it increases unreliability risk for parsing and command behavior over repeated sweeps.

5. Lower-risk signal: in `huamnoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh`, the script posts to xrobot before `train.py` and then starts training.
- If train fails immediately after start-command execution, logs can still be sparse unless those early-fail markers appear.
- Verify will keep classifying as `waiting_for_logs` in those cases unless markers match.

Code evidence:
```112:116: /Users/HanHu/software/motion_rl/humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh
python scripts/xrobot_post_request.py
...
143-151: python humanoid/scripts/train.py ...
```

Objective summary:
- Dispatch itself is green: `status/combo_*.json` shows `success`/`submitted` and each dispatch log contains a `Job '...-huh8' submitted ... successfully!`.
- Verification is the weak link right now:
  - `combo_0001`: unknown at both job and training level.
  - remaining combos: running with no detected training markers yet.
- This is consistent with delayed initialization/log propagation, parser mismatch, or pre-training startup failure; it is not yet a confirmed execution failure.

Open questions / assumptions:
- How long were you polling after dispatch before first check? Current default in verifier is 20 attempts × 30s (10 min).
- Did you also pull raw `fuyao log --job-name=<job> --rank=0 --show-stdout` outputs during the unknown window?
- Do we know if this environment has any log redirection policy that suppresses stdout lines used by current marker set?

Needed follow-ups (in order):
1. Do a raw telemetry pass for all five jobs (bypass parser assumptions) with explicit `fuyao info` and `fuyao log` commands and inspect stderr/stdout directly.
```bash
bash ~/.cursor/scripts/verify_fuyao_jobs.sh --run-root ~/.cursor/tmp/deploy_fuyao_sweep_runs/seed-sweep-20260311-190936-7035 --once
ssh remote.kernel.fuyo "fuyao info -n 'bifrost-2026031210095300-huh8'"
ssh remote.kernel.fuyo "fuyao log --job-name='bifrost-2026031210095300-huh8' --rank=0 --show-stdout"
```

2. Re-run verifier with more patience for startup lag:
```bash
bash ~/.cursor/scripts/verify_fuyao_jobs.sh --run-root ~/.cursor/tmp/deploy_fuyao_sweep_runs/seed-sweep-20260311-190936-7035 --poll-interval 60 --max-attempts 40
```

3. If `combo_0001` remains `"unknown"` after multiple polls, treat it as suspect and isolate/resubmit that combo only for faster triage.

4. Consider a follow-up sweep dispatcher fix to avoid detached-HEAD/upstream warnings (highly recommended before next major run):
- create a per-combo branch/worktree name after adding the worktree so `git rev-parse '@{u}'` can resolve, or ensure the worktree has a valid branch context before `fuyao deploy`.
- This will reduce reproducibility warnings and improve remote metadata consistency.

5. (Optional) broaden verifier markers if needed, but only after confirming what actual training startup strings appear in a healthy run log for this task/environment.

Do you want me to prepare a concrete minimal patch for `prepare_remote_combo_repo` to remove the detached-head warning path in a concurrency-safe way?
